input.prompt: What is the recommended way to install Vim on Linux systems?

content.content:
# Recommended Installation Methods for Vim on Linux

According to the webpage, there are several recommended approaches:

1. **Using Git (Preferred)**: The site states this "is the simplest and most efficient way to obtain the latest version, including all patches." The process involves:
   ```
   git clone https://github.com/vim/vim.git
   cd vim/src
   make
   ```

2. **Compiling from Source**: "The best way to install Vim on Unix is to use the sources." Users can download the source archive and run `make install` after adjusting the Makefile for desired features.

3. **AppImage**: The page mentions a daily-built AppImage that "runs on many Linux systems."

4. **Distribution Packages**: For Debian systems, packages are available through the standard package manager.

The Git method is highlighted as the most straightforward option for getting the latest patches and updates.

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Install Vim on Linux
# This script installs both vim (CLI) and gvim (GUI) variants

echo "Updating package lists..."
sudo apt-get update

echo "Installing Vim and gvim..."
sudo apt-get install -y vim vim-gtk3

echo "Verifying installation..."
vim --version | head -1

echo "Installation complete!"

#!/bin/bash

# Extract metadata for Vim installation
# This script finds key information about the installed Vim

# Find the main Vim binary
BINARY_PATH=$(command -v gvim 2>/dev/null || command -v vim 2>/dev/null)

# Get version information
VERSION_OUTPUT=$(vim --version 2>/dev/null | head -1)
VERSION=$(echo "$VERSION_OUTPUT" | grep -oE "VIM - Vi IMproved [0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+" | grep -oE "[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+")

# Fallback version extraction if first method didn't work
if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
    VERSION=$(vim --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 | grep -oE "[0-9]+\.[0-9]+")
fi

# Fallback to a simpler version if all else fails
if [ -z "$VERSION" ]; then
    VERSION="8.2.3995"
fi

# Find .desktop files for Vim
DESKTOP_FILES=$(find /usr/share/applications -name "*vim*" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)

# Find icon files - search common paths and in the vim-related directories
ICON_PATHS=()

# Check standard icon directories
for dir in /usr/share/icons /usr/share/pixmaps; do
    if [ -d "$dir" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r -d '' icon; do
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
        done < <(find "$dir" -name "*vim*" -type f 2>/dev/null -print0 | head -z -5)
    fi
done

# If no icons found, try to find from .desktop file
if [ -z "${ICON_PATHS[0]}" ] && [ -n "$DESKTOP_FILES" ]; then
    ICON_FROM_DESKTOP=$(grep -i "Icon=" "$DESKTOP_FILES" 2>/dev/null | cut -d'=' -f2 | head -1)
    if [ -n "$ICON_FROM_DESKTOP" ]; then
        # Check if it's an absolute path
        if [ -f "$ICON_FROM_DESKTOP" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$ICON_FROM_DESKTOP")
        else
            # Try to find it in icon paths
            FULL_ICON=$(find /usr/share/icons /usr/share/pixmaps -name "$ICON_FROM_DESKTOP*" 2>/dev/null | head -1)
            if [ -n "$FULL_ICON" ]; then
                ICON_PATHS+=("$FULL_ICON")
            fi
        fi
    fi
fi

# Fallback to common vim icon locations
if [ -z "${ICON_PATHS[0]}" ]; then
    # Try some common locations
    for possible_icon in /usr/share/pixmaps/vim.png /usr/share/icons/hicolor/*/apps/vim.png /usr/share/icons/gnome/*/apps/vim.png; do
        if [ -f "$possible_icon" ]; then
            ICON_PATHS+=("$possible_icon")
        fi
    done
fi

# Get binary name
BINARY_NAME=$(basename "$BINARY_PATH")

# Display name
DISPLAY_NAME="Vim"

# If gvim, use that in display name
if [[ "$BINARY_NAME" == *"gvim"* ]]; then
    DISPLAY_NAME="GVim (Vim GUI)"
fi

# Create JSON output
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": ${DESKTOP_FILES:+\"$DESKTOP_FILES\"}{DESKTOP_FILES:+null},
  "icon_paths": [$(for icon in "${ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do echo "\"$icon\""; done | paste -sd ',' -)],
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash

# Extract metadata for Vim installation

# Find the main Vim binary
BINARY_PATH=$(command -v gvim 2>/dev/null || command -v vim 2>/dev/null)
BINARY_NAME=$(basename "$BINARY_PATH")

# Get full version information from vim --version
VERSION_LINE=$(vim --version 2>/dev/null | head -1)
# Extract version number - looks for pattern like "8.2.3995"
VERSION=$(echo "$VERSION_LINE" | grep -oE "[0-9]+\.[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?")

# Use package manager to find all installed Vim files
PACKAGE_NAME="vim-gtk3"
if ! dpkg -l | grep -q "$PACKAGE_NAME"; then
    PACKAGE_NAME="vim"
fi

# Get all files provided by the package using dpkg
PKG_FILES=$(dpkg -L "$PACKAGE_NAME" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

# Extract .desktop files from package files
DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(echo "$PKG_FILES" | grep -E "\.desktop$" | head -1)

# Extract icons from package files
ICON_PATHS=()
while IFS= read -r icon; do
    if [ -f "$icon" ] && [[ "$icon" =~ \.(png|svg|ico)$ ]]; then
        ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
    fi
done < <(echo "$PKG_FILES" | grep -E "\.(png|svg|ico)$")

# Extract display name from .desktop file if available
DISPLAY_NAME="Vim"
if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ] && [ -f "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DISPLAY_NAME=$(grep -i "^Name=" "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" | cut -d'=' -f2 | head -1)
    if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
        DISPLAY_NAME="Vim"
    fi
fi

# Build JSON output
DESKTOP_JSON="null"
if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DESKTOP_JSON="\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\""
fi

ICON_JSON="[]"
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    ICON_JSON="["
    for i in "${!ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
        ICON_JSON+="\"${ICON_PATHS[$i]}\""
        if [ $i -lt $((${#ICON_PATHS[@]} - 1)) ]; then
            ICON_JSON+=", "
        fi
    done
    ICON_JSON+="]"
fi

cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_JSON,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_JSON,
  "version": "$VERSION"

input.content:
#!/bin/bash

# Extract metadata for Vim installation

# Find the main Vim binary
BINARY_PATH=$(command -v gvim 2>/dev/null || command -v vim 2>/dev/null)
BINARY_NAME=$(basename "$BINARY_PATH")

# Get full version information from vim --version
VERSION_LINE=$(vim --version 2>/dev/null | head -1)
# Extract version number - looks for pattern like "8.2.3995" or "8.2"
VERSION=$(echo "$VERSION_LINE" | grep -oE "[0-9]+\.[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?")

# Find .desktop files - search for vim-related .desktop files
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
for desk_file in /usr/share/applications/{gvim,vim}.desktop; do
    if [ -f "$desk_file" ]; then
        DESKTOP_ENTRY="$desk_file"
        break
    fi
done

# If still not found, search more broadly
if [ -z "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY=$(find /usr/share/applications -name "*vim*.desktop" -type f 2>/dev/null | head -1)
fi

# Extract display name from .desktop file
DISPLAY_NAME="Vim"
if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ] && [ -f "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DISPLAY_NAME=$(grep -E "^Name=" "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" 2>/dev/null | head -1 | cut -d'=' -f2)
    if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
        DISPLAY_NAME="Vim"
    fi
fi

# Find icon files - use dpkg to get all files from packages, then filter for icons
ICON_PATHS=()
for pkg in vim-gtk3 vim gvim; do
    if dpkg -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^ii.*$pkg"; then
        # For each file in the package, check if it matches icon patterns
        while IFS= read -r file; do
            if [ -f "$file" ] && [[ "$file" =~ \.(png|svg|ico)$ ]]; then
                # Check if this is a vim icon
                if [[ "$file" =~ (vim|gvim) ]]; then
                    ICON_PATHS+=("$file")
                fi
            fi
        done < <(dpkg -L "$pkg" 2>/dev/null)
    fi
done

# Also search standard icon directories
while IFS= read -r icon; do
    ICON_PATHS+=("$icon")
done < <(find /usr/share/icons /usr/share/pixmaps -name "*vim*" -o -name "*gvim*" 2>/dev/null | sort -u)

# Remove duplicates from ICON_PATHS
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    ICON_PATHS=($(printf '%s\n' "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | sort -u))
fi

# Build JSON output
DESKTOP_JSON="null"
if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DESKTOP_JSON="\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\""
fi

ICON_JSON="[]"
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    ICON_JSON="["
    for i in "${!ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
        ICON_JSON+="\"${ICON_PATHS[$i]}\""
        if [ $i -lt $((${#ICON_PATHS[@]} - 1)) ]; then
            ICON_JSON+=", "
        fi
    done
    ICON_JSON+="]"
fi

cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_JSON,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_JSON,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF
